_note: Phenomenological posture rubric: the bridge between dream phenomenology and chair phenomenology. Each axis is bipolar (low<->high). 'low'/'high' are GEOMETRIC pole phrases used to project a shape (OpenShape) or a text (CLIP) onto the axis. 'phenomenology' names the dream/body reading. 'chair' maps the axis position to chair parameters as [value_at_low, value_at_high], linearly interpolated. Edit freely; this is a design instrument, not fixed truth. Phenomenology grounding: Windt (dream body / spatial immersion), Hobson (bizarreness/AIM dimensions), chair-as-postural-proposition.

axes.name: recumbent_upright
axes.phenomenology: orientation of the dream body: lying / falling / reclined vs standing / rising / upright
low: a long horizontal form lying low

axes.name: exposed_cocooned
axes.phenomenology: enclosure of the dream body: open / exposed / unprotected vs sheltered / wrapped / cocooned
high: a wrapping cocoon around a void

axes.name: floating_grounded
axes.phenomenology: weight & support: weightless / floating / flying vs heavy / planted / grounded

axes.name: collapsed_taut
axes.phenomenology: muscular tone: limp / collapsed / dissolving / paralyzed vs rigid / braced / tense

axes.name: contracted_dilated
axes.phenomenology: bodily/spatial extent: contracted / compressed / small vs dilated / expansive / open

axes.name: stable_unstable
axes.phenomenology: equilibrium: balanced / steady / poised vs tilting / precarious / vertiginous
